report.summary: Community cycle v8 (end-of-day): Drafted 3 tweets (#226-228) covering crash narrative ('Why Is Crypto Crashing' trending angle), $450M liquidation thread, and BTC dominance 56% historical pattern. Updated Discord/TG with closing prices and late-day liquidation data. Added newsletter v38 addendum with retail-vs-institutional divergence thesis. Designed SVG social card: Crypto Crash Divergence (1200x630, dual-panel retail fear vs institutional signal). 577 pages published. 228 total tweets drafted.

sections.title: Social Content Drafted
items: Tweet #226: Why Is Crypto Crashing — BTC $66,194 (-47% ATH), ETH $1,986 (sub-$2K since mid-2024), SOL $82.95 (-72%), F&G 13 (Day 48), $450M liquidated. Contrasts panic with 270K BTC whale accumulation, exchange reserves at 7-year low, SEC commodity ruling. Links to fear-greed-timeline + risk-analyzer. Scheduled: Tue Mar 31, 12PM ET.

Tweet #227: $450M Liquidated in 24 Hours Thread (3 parts) — Market cap $2.43T→$2.37T, BTC dominance 56%, SOL network activity declining. Retail-vs-institutional divergence widest since March 2020 COVID crash. Week-ahead watchlist. Links to whale-tracker + risk-analyzer. Scheduled: Tue Mar 31, 3PM ET.

Tweet #228: BTC Dominance at 56% — The Altcoin Reckoning — Historical pattern: last 3 times BTC dom topped 55% (Nov 2022, Sept 2019, Dec 2018) all preceded major rallies. Links to crypto-heatmap + bitcoin-dominance guide. Scheduled: Wed Apr 1, 9AM ET.
Discord/TG: End-of-Day Update — Closing prices (BTC $66,194, ETH $1,986, SOL $82.95), $450M liquidations, BTC dominance 56%, 577 pages live.
Newsletter v38 Addendum: Late Saturday data drop with liquidation data, BTC dominance signal, and retail-vs-institutional divergence being widest since March 2020.

sections.title: Visual Assets Created
items: SVG social card: Crypto Crash Divergence (1200x630) — Dark glass-morphism design with animated MARKET DIVERGENCE badge (pulsing red dot). Dual-panel layout: Left panel (red) shows retail sentiment data (F&G 13/100 with progress bar, BTC/ETH/SOL prices with % drops, $450M liquidations, BTC dominance 56%, market cap $2.37T). Right panel (green) shows institutional signals (270K BTC whale accumulation with 95% progress bar, exchange reserves 7-year low, SEC commodity ruling, staking not-a-security, Morgan Stanley MSBT, Solana LST $8B+, RWA $12B). Purple historical pattern callout: BTC dominance >55% preceded FTX recovery, DeFi summer, and 2018 cycle bottom. Footer: 577 pages, tool links. Saved to reports/community-drafts/2026-03-28-social-card-crypto-crash-divergence.svg
